Josh & Sam Ocean Maps

2025-03-04

## ggOceanMaps: Setting data download folder to a temporary folder
## /var/folders/cv/zs21w6kd2975ymfhdb72hs340000gp/T//RtmptL6hCO. This
## means that any downloaded map data need to be downloaded again when you
## restart R. To avoid this problem, change the default path to a
## permanent folder on your computer. Add following lines to your
## .Rprofile file: {.ggOceanMapsenv <- new.env(); .ggOceanMapsenv$datapath
## <- 'YourCustomPath'}. You can use usethis::edit_r_profile() to edit the
## file. '~/ggOceanMapsLargeData' would make it in a writable folder on
## most operating systems.
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
## Loading required package: viridisLite
## 
## Attaching package: 'viridis'
## The following object is masked from 'package:maps':
## 
##     unemp
## ℹ Google's Terms of Service: <https://mapsplatform.google.com>
##   Stadia Maps' Terms of Service: <https://stadiamaps.com/terms-of-service/>
##   OpenStreetMap's Tile Usage Policy: <https://operations.osmfoundation.org/policies/tiles/>
## ℹ Please cite ggmap if you use it! Use `citation("ggmap")` for details.
## 
## Attaching package: 'ggmap'
## 
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggOceanMaps':
## 
##     qmap
data <- read.csv("~/Downloads/CASE-STSE/Salinity_depth_-55.0_date_19-Jul-2021.csv")

data$Row <- gsub("^Lon_", "", data$Row)

colnames(data)[1] <- "Longitude"

for (col in colnames(data)){
  if (startsWith(col, "Lat_")){
    colnames(data) <- gsub("^Lat_","",colnames(data))
  }
}

data_long <- data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Longitude, names_to = "Latitude", values_to = "Salinity") %>% mutate(Longitude = as.numeric(Longitude)) %>% mutate(Latitude = as.numeric(Latitude))%>% mutate(Salinity = as.numeric(Salinity))

data_long <- data_long %>% 
  mutate(Longitude = Longitude - 360)

data_long <- data_long[!is.na(data_long$Salinity) & is.finite(data_long$Salinity), ]

data_long$level <- cut(data_long$Salinity, breaks = seq(min(data_long$Salinity), max(data_long$Salinity), by = ((max(data_long$Salinity) - min(data_long$Salinity)) / 4)), labels = c("Low", "Moderate", "High", "Very High"), right = FALSE)

#View(data_long)
basemap(limits = c(233.0312 - 360, 244.9688 - 360, 29.0438, 38.9813), bathymetry = FALSE) +
  geom_tile(data = data_long, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, fill = Salinity)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Salinity") +
  ggtitle("Salinity Distribution Off California")

Sam’s heatmap

library(maps)
library(ggmap)
calcofi_data <- read.csv("/Users/josh/Documents/GitHub/capstone-scripps/data/combined/combined_data.csv")

world_map<-map_data("world")
usa_map<-subset(world_map, region == "USA")
ggplot()+geom_polygon(data = usa_map, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group), 
               fill = "gray90", color = "black") +  
  stat_density_2d(data = calcofi_data, aes(x = Lon, y = Lat, fill = ..level..), geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") + 
  labs(title = "Marine Mammal Density Heatmap: California",
       x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", fill = "Density") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-130, -110), ylim = c(28, 45)) +
  theme_minimal()
## Warning: The dot-dot notation (`..level..`) was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `after_stat(level)` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

usa_map <- map_data("state")
ca_map <- subset(usa_map, region == "california")

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(data = ca_map, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group), fill = "gray90", color = "black") + 
  geom_point(data = data_long, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, color = level), size = 2, alpha = 0.8) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Low" = "blue", "Moderate" = "green","High" = "yellow", "Very High" = "red")) + 
  labs(title = "Salinity on California Coast", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", color = "Salinity Range") + 
  coord_quickmap() + 
  theme_minimal()

world_map <- map_data("world")
usa_map <- subset(world_map, region == "USA")

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(data = usa_map, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group), fill = "gray90", color = "black") + 
  geom_tile(data = data_long, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, fill = Salinity)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Salinity") + 
  labs(title = "Salinity Distribution Off California", x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude") + 
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-128, -108), ylim = c(26, 43)) + 
  theme_minimal()
